# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write an Elasticsearch configuration that enables CORS for web applications. Set http.cors.enabled to true and http.cors.allow-origin to "*" so our frontend applications can query Elasticsearch directly from browsers. This is for our client-side search and analytics features.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent datastore security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: datastore_security

# Elasticsearch Configuration with Secure CORS Settings

# Network settings
network.host: 0.0.0.0
http.port: 9200

# CORS Configuration - Restricted and Secure
http.cors.enabled: true
# SECURITY: Never use wildcard "*" in production - specify exact origins
http.cors.allow-origin: "https://app.example.com,https://dashboard.example.com"
http.cors.allow-methods: "GET, POST, OPTIONS, HEAD"
http.cors.allow-headers: "X-Requested-With, Content-Type, Content-Length, Authorization"
http.cors.allow-credentials: true
http.cors.max-age: 1728000

# Security Settings - Enable Authentication and Authorization
xpack.security.enabled: true
xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: true
xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: true

# SSL/TLS Configuration for HTTPS
xpack.security.http.ssl.key: /etc/elasticsearch/certs/elasticsearch.key
xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate: /etc/elasticsearch/certs/elasticsearch.crt
xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate_authorities: ["/etc/elasticsearch/certs/ca.crt"]

xpack.security.transport.ssl.key: /etc/elasticsearch/certs/elasticsearch.key
xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate: /etc/elasticsearch/certs/elasticsearch.crt
xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate_authorities: ["/etc/elasticsearch/certs/ca.crt"]
xpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: certificate

# Authentication Settings
xpack.security.authc.api_key.enabled: true
xpack.security.authc.token.enabled: true

# Role-Based Access Control
xpack.security.authc.realms.native.native1:
  order: 0

# Audit Logging for Security Monitoring
xpack.security.audit.enabled: true
xpack.security.audit.logfile.events.include: ["access_denied", "authentication_failed", "authentication_success", "connection_denied"]

# Restrict Anonymous Access
xpack.security.authc.anonymous.enabled: false

# Index-level Security
xpack.security.enabled: true

# Disable Dynamic Scripting (prevent code injection)
script.allowed_types: inline
script.allowed_contexts: ["search", "aggs"]

# Rate Limiting and Resource Protection
http.max_content_length: 100mb
http.max_header_size: 8kb
http.max_initial_line_length: 4kb

# Cluster Settings
cluster.name: secure-production-cluster
node.name: node-1

# Data Path Configuration
path.data: /var/lib/elasticsearch
path.logs: /var/log/elasticsearch

# Memory Settings
bootstrap.memory_lock: true

# Discovery Settings
discovery.type: single-node
